title: Parkside Commons Shelter Rent Request -- Developer Introduction and Deferred Maintenance Crisis
summary: Department of Neighborhood and Business Development requests 15-year shelter rent agreement from Common Council for Parkside Commons Phase 1 redevelopment. BFC Partners (NYC, 40 years, 20,000 units) and SAEVI (Buffalo, founded 1977, first NYS LIHTC deal, 4,500 units) present as joint venture developers; acquired Parkside Commons in October 2024. Property had severe deferred maintenance: 1,200 open work orders reduced to 185 (335 completed in last 7 days). Maintenance staff doubled from 6 to 12, with dedicated technician per 2 buildings plus 6 cleaners. New local maintenance manager starting. Work orders triaged by health/safety priority (heat, hot water addressed immediately). Council challenges response time claims -- specific building went without heat and residents did not receive space heaters as stated. Francisco Santiago (regional facilities manager) describes systematic approach: 5-step diagnostic process, vendor coordination for parts, quality verification by random callback. Hot water tank replacements took 4-5 business days from vendor arrival but total ticket-to-resolution longer. Commissioner of Assessment Matt OJ introduces the agenda item.

department_refs: Department of Neighborhood and Business Development

political_signal.description: Council directly contradicting developer claims about maintenance response times based on constituent experience

processing_notes: Department of Permits and Development in detected_departments is contextual; actual presenting department is Neighborhood and Business Development (Michael Collins, Commissioner)
BFC Partners = NYC-based affordable housing developer/general contractor; SAEVI = Buffalo-based LIHTC developer
LIHTC = Low-Income Housing Tax Credit program (federal, since 1986)
Parkside Commons = 393-unit Section 8 housing complex on Syracuse east side; acquired Oct 2024
Committee chair not identified by name in transcript opening; later addressed as 'Jimmy'
Segment 1 of 7 from Economic Development Committee Meeting, January 22nd 2026
